description: Unified authentication modes for public deployment and company intranet deployment.
implemented_auth_modes_note: Only these values are legal AUTH_MODE settings today; per-deploy-mode combinations are whitelisted by config/contracts/deployment_modes.json modes.*.allowed_auth_modes and enforced at startup. Modes marked status=planned below are contract placeholders with zero implementation.

local.purpose: Local development and early public beta.

public_password.purpose: Public internet deployment with normal login.

public_oidc.purpose: Public internet deployment through OAuth/OIDC provider.
public_oidc.status: planned; use the generic oidc mode today

oidc.purpose: Generic OIDC authorization code flow with PKCE for cloud/extranet SSO.
oidc.status: implemented via GET /api/auth/oidc/start and GET /api/auth/oidc/callback; requires OIDC_CLIENT_ID and OIDC_ISSUER or explicit endpoints; supports configured claim mapping, guarded next redirect, friendly auth_error redirect, and automatic workspace membership
oidc.id_token_verification: Once the token endpoint returns an id_token it is verified as a whole: alg=none is always rejected. When a JWKS is available (OIDC_JWKS_URI env, or jwks_uri from issuer discovery) RS256/RS384/RS512 signatures are verified (stdlib RSASSA-PKCS1-v1_5, kid-matched JWK; non-RS* algs are rejected when JWKS is configured). Without a JWKS, iss/aud/exp are strictly validated and nonce must be present and match. The userinfo main path still enforces the id_token nonce; userinfo itself needs no nonce because it is a server-side direct TLS call bound by PKCE.

intranet_header.purpose: Company intranet deployment behind trusted gateway/portal.
intranet_header.trusted_proxy_rule: When AUTH_TRUSTED_PROXY_CIDRS is non-empty, identity headers are only trusted when the direct peer (request.client.host) falls inside the CIDR whitelist; requests from untrusted peers are treated as anonymous (401). Empty config keeps the legacy trust-any-peer behavior and logs a startup warning; invalid CIDRs fail startup.

intranet_oidc.purpose: Company intranet deployment through enterprise OIDC.

intranet_saml.purpose: Company intranet deployment through enterprise SAML.

account_lifecycle_tables.user_invites: Admin-created invite codes with role_code, workspace targets, expiry, revoke and acceptance state.
account_lifecycle_tables.password_reset_tokens: Hashed reset tokens only; plaintext token is never stored.
account_lifecycle_tables.login_attempts: Username + IP success/failure events for local rate limiting and audit support.

password_flows.invite_accept: Anonymous users can accept a pending invite code, create a local password account, receive the configured global role and explicit workspace memberships, and get a session cookie.
password_flows.change_password: Only users with external_provider=local can change password with current_password. OIDC/intranet_header and other external identities return Password is managed externally; frontend /account must hide local password fields for those identities. must_change_password users may only call /api/auth/me, /api/auth/logout and /api/auth/password/change until complete.
password_flows.forgot_password: Always returns 200; without SMTP it writes audit only and does not create a token.
password_flows.admin_reset: Super admins can issue one temporary password and set users.status=must_change_password only for external_provider=local users; external identities are managed by their provider.

invite_public_page.render_rule: Only pending invites render the account creation form. accepted, revoked and expired invites must render readable status explanations and no submit form.
invite_public_page.client_validation: username and display_name are required; password must be at least 8 characters and match confirmPassword before calling the API.
invite_public_page.error_mapping: Invite not found, Invite is accepted/revoked/expired, Username already exists and HTTP 422 must be shown as user-facing messages.

oidc_claim_mapping.missing_external_id_error: OIDC claims missing configured external id claim: <claim>

auto_membership_mapping.role_update_rule: add missing memberships and upgrade to the configured role when higher; never downgrade existing workspace roles
auto_membership_mapping.runtime_visibility: GET /api/meta/runtime exposes sanitized env default_workspaces and department_workspaces for read-only deployment-layer display.

workspace_edit_api.rule: Stored workspace department mappings are merged with env mappings during oidc and intranet_header auto provisioning; higher workspace_role wins and existing manual roles are never downgraded.

login_redirect.allowed_next: same-app relative paths starting with / but not //
login_redirect.local_password_login: frontend /login honors the redirect query from router guard
login_redirect.oidc_error_redirect: Browser OIDC start/callback failures redirect to /login?auth_error=<code> instead of exposing provider/backend details

login_rate_limit.client_ip_rule: The rate-limit IP shares the trusted-proxy decision with header identity: X-Forwarded-For (first hop) is only honored when the direct peer is inside AUTH_TRUSTED_PROXY_CIDRS; otherwise (including when the CIDR list is unset) the direct peer IP is used, so rotating forged XFF values cannot bypass the 5-failures/15-minutes window by default.

session_hardening.cookie_secure_env: AUTH_SESSION_COOKIE_SECURE; defaults to true when APP_ENV=production, but local HTTP Docker acceptance may set false
session_hardening.payload_nonce: users.updated_at serialized as sv; changing password or admin reset invalidates old cookies

secret_rotation.format: comma-separated secret list; when set it overrides the single-value AUTH_SESSION_SECRET
secret_rotation.semantics: The first entry signs newly issued session cookies; every entry can verify existing cookies. To rotate without logging users out, prepend the new secret and keep the old one at the tail; removing a secret from the list immediately invalidates cookies signed with it.
secret_rotation.startup_check: The fail-fast secret check passes when either AUTH_SESSION_SECRET or AUTH_SESSION_SECRETS is non-empty (Settings syncs the first rotation entry back into auth_session_secret so all single-value read points see the signing secret).

session_hardening.startup_self_check: APP_ENV=production requires DATABASE_URL. AUTH_SESSION_SECRET (or the AUTH_SESSION_SECRETS rotation list) must be non-empty for every auth mode (local/public_password/oidc/intranet_header all sign session cookies); the check runs in validate_deploy_settings shared by the API, scheduler and worker entrypoints, with the original public_password check in main.py kept as a fallback.

setup_flow.create_endpoint: POST /api/setup -> first local super_admin; only allowed while users table is empty
setup_flow.bootstrap_compatibility: AUTH_BOOTSTRAP_ADMIN_PASSWORD still creates the first admin for unattended deployments; when present, setup status is false

workspace_membership_rule.explicit_workspace_code_rule: Business list and mutation APIs that operate on workspace content must receive workspace_code from the caller; they must not silently fall back to a tenant-specific workspace.

viewer: workspace sections and label policy read
ingestion and recommendation run reads when workspace_code is provided
daily report item reactions, ratings and comments when workspace feedback_policy allows viewer feedback
report format list and rendition export
export job list/detail/trace when workspace_code or job workspace is known
member: daily and weekly report item edit
daily report item reactions, ratings and comments when viewer feedback is closed
admin: workspace member list/add/remove in that workspace
workspace member owner downgrade or owner removal requires confirm_dangerous_change and last owner is still blocked
custom source create/update/link in that workspace
news normalization, ingestion, backfill, recommendation and daily pipeline runs
super_admin_only: global user list without workspace_code, role and invite administration

rollback_request.audit_log_ids: 1-20 audit log ids from supported permission change actions
rollback_request.confirm_dangerous_change: Required when a rollback could downgrade or remove an owner; backend still blocks the last super_admin and last workspace owner

rules: Supported audit logs must include before/after snapshots sufficient to explain the diff and restore the previous value.
Rollback writes audit_logs.action=identity.permission_rollback with the source audit log id and before/after rollback snapshots.
Rollback cannot remove the last active super_admin or the last enabled workspace owner.
The /users policy view may render diffs and batch rollback only from this API; it must not infer recoverability from raw audit JSON alone.

rbac_rule: External identity proves who the user is; InfoWatchtower roles decide what the user can do.
